questions.question: A metallic cube of side 12 cm is melted to form 27 smaller cubes. Find the side of each smaller cube.
questions.answer: Volume large cube=12³=1728 cm³; Each small cube=1728/27=64 ⇒ Side=∛64=4 cm
answer_outline: Divide by number of smaller cubes
Take cube root to find side

questions.question: A solid sphere of radius 21 cm is melted to form smaller spheres each of radius 7 cm. Find number of smaller spheres formed.
questions.answer: Volume large sphere=4/3 π21³=4/3×3.14×9261≈38716.68; Volume small sphere=4/3×3.14×343≈1436.03; Number=38716.68/1436.03≈27 spheres
answer_outline: Divide to get number of spheres

questions.question: A solid metallic cylinder of radius 7 cm and height 24 cm is melted to form smaller spheres each of radius 3 cm. Find number of spheres.
questions.answer: Volume cylinder=πr²h=3.14×49×24≈3692.64; Volume sphere=4/3 π3³≈113.04; Number=3692.64/113.04≈32 spheres
answer_outline: Divide volumes for number of spheres

questions.question: A solid sphere of radius 14 cm is hollowed out to form a spherical shell of inner radius 7 cm. Find volume of the shell.
A solid hemisphere of radius 7 cm is placed on top of a cylinder of same radius and height 14 cm. Find total surface area of combination.
questions.answer: CSA hemisphere=2πr²=2×3.14×49≈307.72; CSA cylinder=2πrh=2×3.14×7×14≈615.44; Total SA=CSA hemisphere + CSA cylinder=307.72+615.44≈923.16 cm²

questions.question: A cone of radius 7 cm and height 24 cm is hollowed from a cylinder of same base and height. Find volume of remaining solid.
questions.answer: Volume cylinder=πr²h=3.14×49×24≈3692.64; Volume cone=(1/3)πr²h≈1230.88; Remaining volume=3692.64-1230.88≈2461.76 cm³

questions.question: A solid sphere is melted to form a right circular cone of height 24 cm and base radius 14 cm. Find radius of original sphere.
A solid cube of side 10 cm is converted into a sphere. Find radius of sphere.
The diameter of a cylindrical tank is 7 m and its height is 10 m. Find the volume of water it can hold.
A right circular cone of radius 14 cm and height 24 cm is completely filled with water. Find total surface area of water in contact.
A metallic sphere of radius 7 cm is melted to form a hollow cylinder of radius 7 cm and height 6 cm. Find thickness of cylinder wall.
answer_outline: Use cylinder hollow volume formula π(R²-r²)h
Solve for outer radius R, subtract inner radius to get thickness

questions.question: A cylindrical pipe of outer radius 7 cm, inner radius 6 cm, and length 14 cm is made of metal. Find volume of metal used.
answer_outline: Compute difference of squares of radii

questions.question: A metallic cube of side 21 cm is melted to form smaller cones each of radius 3 cm and height 7 cm. Find number of cones formed.
questions.answer: Volume cube=9261; Volume cone=(1/3)πr²h=(1/3)×3.14×9×7≈65.94; Number of cones=9261/65.94≈140 cones
answer_outline: Divide to get number of cones

questions.question: A cone of height 24 cm and base radius 7 cm is cut into two parts by a plane parallel to base such that smaller cone volume is 1/8 of original. Find height of smaller cone.
A cylindrical tank of radius 7 m is 6 m deep. Find the cost of painting its inner curved surface at Rs 5 per m².
A conical vessel of radius 7 cm and height 24 cm is full of water. Find mass of water if 1 cm³ weighs 1 g.
The radius of a sphere increases from 7 cm to 14 cm. Find ratio of their surface areas.
Find the ratio of volumes of two spheres of radii 7 cm and 14 cm.
A solid hemisphere of radius 14 cm is melted to form small spheres of radius 7 cm. Find number of spheres formed.
questions.answer: Volume hemisphere=(2/3)πr³=(2/3)×3.14×2744≈5735.36; Volume small sphere=1436.03; Number=5735.36/1436.03≈4 spheres
answer_outline: Divide to get number of spheres

questions.question: A cylindrical pillar has diameter 14 cm and height 3 m. Find cost of painting lateral surface at Rs 10 per m².
The height of a cylinder is equal to diameter. Find ratio of its curved surface area to total surface area.
questions.answer: Let radius r, height h=2r; CSA=2πrh=4πr², TSA=2πr(h+r)=2πr(3r)=6πr²; Ratio=CSA:TSA=4πr²:6πr²=2:3
answer_outline: Express h in terms of r

questions.question: A cylindrical pipe has inner radius 14 cm, outer radius 15 cm, and length 2 m. Find volume of metal used.
A sphere of radius 14 cm is melted to form a cylinder of same radius. Find height of cylinder.
questions.answer: Volume sphere=4/3 πr³=11494.08; Volume cylinder=πr²h=3.14×196×h=11494.08 ⇒ h≈58.5 cm

questions.question: A cylindrical tank of radius 7 m and height 10 m is full of water. Find surface area of tank in contact with water.
A metallic sphere of radius 7 cm is melted to form a cylindrical rod of radius 3.5 cm. Find height of rod.
